core_concept: Elements of Art: Color, Line, Sculpture, Architecture
facts: A line can be straight, curved, thick, or thin; artists use lines to draw shapes and make pictures.
A sculpture is three-dimensional, which means it has height, width, and depth, so you can see it from all sides.
Architecture is the art of designing buildings; houses, schools, and castles are all examples of architecture.
misconceptions: A building is not art because it is not a drawing or painting.
